LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER 345 CALIFORNIA L.P.'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT. NO APPEARANCES. THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: TRANSFERRED FOR HEARING IN DEPT. 501 ON APRIL 19, 2021 AT 9:30 A.M. THAT DEPARTMENT HEARS ALL REAL PROPERTY AND HOUSING MATTERS INCLUDING ACTIONS FOR BREACH OF LEASE. (SEE SF LOCAL RULE 8.10.) THE MOVING PARTY SHALL IMMEDIATELY PROVIDE HARD COURTESY COPIES OF ITS FILINGS WITH DEPARTMENT 501. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PETITIONER 345 CALIFORNIA L.P.'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER ORDER FOR ISSUANCE OF WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $927,188.17 IN PAST DUE RENT. PETITIONER SHALL POST BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $30,000.00. MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE AS TO THE RENT BECOMING DUE UNDER THE LEASE FOR PETITIONER TO SEEK ATTACHMENT AS THE RENT BECOMES DUE WITH SHOWING OF ACTUAL MITIGATION EFFORTS AFTER TENANT PROPERLY SURRENDERS POSSESSION. MOTION IS FURTHER DENIED AS TO THE ESTIMATED ATTORNEY'S FEES AS NO REASONABLE FACTUAL BASIS FOR THE ESTIMATED AMOUNT WAS PROVIDED TO THE COURT TO MAKE AN APPROPRIATE DETERMINATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Jerod Hendrickson; Sheppard-Uziel Law Firm; 415-296-0900; appearing via Zoom on behalf of respondent Simpler Postage, Inc.

Petitioner contests the court's tentative ruling. The court and the parties discuss petitioner's reply brief and it's supporting declaration filed on August 6, 2021. The parties stipulate to a two week continuance. Both sides waive notice. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows: PLAINTIFF 345 CALIFORNIA L.P.'S NOTICE OF APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND WRIT OF ATTACHMENT is continued to August 24, 2021 for respondent to file opposition, if any, to petitioner's reply brief. Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment
Off Calendar Apr-19-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Christopher Gribble; Farella Brown & Martel LLP; 415-954-4400; appearing via Zoom on behalf of petitioner 345 California LP.
Jerod Hendrickson; Sheppard Uziel Law Firm; 415-296-0900; appearing via CourtCall on behalf of respondent Simpler Postage, Inc.

Petitioner contests the tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court rules as follows: PETITIONER 345 CALIFORNIA L.P.'s Notice Of Application And Hearing For Right To Attach Order Order For Issuance Of Writ Of Attachment is GRANTED in the amount of $927,188.17 in past due rent. Petitioner shall post bond in the amount of $30,000.00. Motion is denied without prejudice as to the rent becoming due under the lease for Petitioner to seek attachment as the rent becomes due with showing of actual mitigation efforts after tenant properly surrenders possession. Motion is further denied as to the estimated attorney's fees as no reasonable factual basis for the estimated amount was provided to the Court to make an appropriate determination. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
